title: hystrix duthiei subsp. longe-aristata
intro: Hystrix duthiei subsp. longe-aristata is a subspecies of plant within the genus Hystrix, commonly known as the porcupine grass, characterized by its spiny stems and unique appearance. This plant is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Himalayan region, where it is valued for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Hystrix duthiei subsp. longe-aristata include its potential to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, decoctions, or tinctures, which can be consumed as a tea or used topically to apply its medicinal properties.
